questions.question: Which article of the Constitution of India provides for a Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: The Article 280 of the Constitution of India provides for a Finance Commission as a quasi judicial body. The Constitution empowers the Parliament of India to determine the qualifications of members of the commission and the manner in which they should be selected.

questions.question: Which part of the Constitution makes provisions with regard to the electoral system in India?
questions.explanation: The Articles 324 to 329 in Part XV of the Constitution of India makes provisions with regard to the electoral system in our India. The Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for an independent Election Commission in order to ensure free and fair elections in the country.

questions.question: The CAG’s (Duties, Powers and Conditions of Service) Act was amended in which year to separate accounts from audit in the Central government?
questions.explanation: The CAG’s (Duties, Powers and Conditions of Service) Act enacted by the Parliament in 1971 was amended in the year 1976 to separate accounts from audit in the Central government.

questions.question: The board of cooperative societies may be superseded or kept under suspension in which of the following cases?1. Of its persistent default2. Of negligence in the performance of its duties3. Of there being a stalement in the constitution or functions of the boardSelect the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Some of the cases in which the board of cooperative societies may be superseded or kept under suspension are: (1) Of its persistent default, (2) Of negligence in the performance of its duties, (3) Of there being a stalement in the constitution or functions of the board etc.

questions.question: The State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission will hear complaints of how much worth?

options.label: A
options.text: more than Rs 1 lakh but less than Rs 10 lakh

options.label: B
options.text: more than Rs 10 crore but less than Rs 50 crore

options.label: C
options.text: more than Rs 1 crore but less than Rs 10 crore

options.label: D
options.text: more than Rs 5 crore but less than Rs 10 crore

answer.label: C
answer.text: more than Rs 1 crore but less than Rs 10 crore

questions.explanation: The State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission provided in Consumer Protection Act, 2019 will hear complaints of more than Rs 1 crore but less than Rs 10 crore.

questions.question: The Indian Institute of Management Act was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian Institute of Management Act of 2017 declared the Indian Institute of Management as institutions of national importance. It enabled them to offer degrees and further make substantial changes in their administration.

questions.question: The Child and Adolescent Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Child and Adolescent Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act was passed by the Parliament of India in the year 1986. It was enacted on 23 December 1986 to prohibit private, government or semi-government companies, organizations, civil departments or child’s family from employing a child or adolescents in any occupation or process.

questions.question: How many Fundamental Rights are mentioned in Indian Constitution ?
questions.explanation: There are 6 fundamental rights under Part III of Indian constitution. They are as follows: Right to equality; Right to freedom; Right against exploitation; Right to freedom of religion; Cultural and Educational right; Right to constitutional remedies.

questions.question: Which one of the following is an item included in the list of Fundamental Duties of an Indian citizen in the Constitution ?

options.label: B
options.text: To develop scientific temper, humanism and the spirit of enquiry and reform.

options.label: C
options.text: To pay all taxes to government regularly and correctly.

options.label: D
options.text: Not to assault any public servant in the (during) performance of his duties.

answer.label: B
answer.text: To develop scientific temper, humanism and the spirit of enquiry and reform.

questions.explanation: The fundamental duty is: to develop the scientific temper, humanism and the spirit of inquiry and reform. There are 11 fundamental duties under Article 51A of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Other than ‘Right to life’ which other right is mentioned in article 21 of Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 21 of the Constitution of India, 1950 provides that, “No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.” It has a much wider meaning which includes right to live with human dignity, right to livelihood, right to food, right to health, right to pollution free air, etc.
